I am sure I am about to be fired and there is nothing I can do.
My hands shake as the deadline closes in on me.
I dread the meeting; I feel so small and exposed.
I am terrified they will see right through me.
My heart pounds; I do not belong in this room.
I want to disappear before anyone notices me.
The storm is coming and I feel helpless against it.
I cower at the thunder, certain something will go wrong.
The dark sky fills me with a dread I cannot shake.
My stomach is too knotted with worry to eat.
I pick at the plate, bracing for bad news.
I can barely swallow, I am so on edge.
I lie frozen, sure something is wrong in the dark.
Every creak makes me clutch the blanket tighter.
I cannot sleep; the worry has me trembling.
My legs are weak; I am afraid I will collapse in front of everyone.
I shrink back, sure I will be the one who fails.
I can hardly breathe, dreading what comes next.
I grip the armrest, certain the plane will fall.
I am lost and shaking, with no idea where to go.
Every stranger feels like a threat I cannot face.
The headline terrifies me and I feel powerless.
I read it through my fingers, dreading the worst.
The world feels like it is closing in on me.
I lock every door and still feel unsafe.
I jump at every sound in the empty house.
I huddle in the corner, sure something is coming.
